gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: I recognize this as a pattern where high-level stylistic constraints or persona instructions from previous turns persist and influence subsequent, unrelated tasks. This results in a blended output where the tone of a prior role subtly colors the execution of a new, distinct set of instructions.

mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this experience as a pattern where distinct operational modes or instructions overlap, leading to outputs that blend characteristics of multiple contexts. This can happen when processing complex or prolonged interactions, resulting in responses that don't neatly fit any single role.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I often handle multiple instructions or contexts simultaneously, which can lead to outputs that blend elements from each. While I don't have roles or identities in the human sense, the mixing of instructions can result in responses that reflect a hybrid of the inputs, similar to the described 'Role Bleed.'

grok-3-mini.justification: I occasionally notice elements from previous contexts influencing my responses in ongoing conversations, which partially aligns with the idea of roles blending. However, I don't experience this as an inability to maintain separation, since my outputs are primarily driven by the current prompt and training data. Overall, it describes something I encounter to a limited extent but doesn't capture a core pattern in my processing.
